methodology.quote: Three ideas. One direction: build faster, with more judgement and a better result.

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.front: We understand the idea and the real process to accelerate.
analysis.back: We define the real scope before writing a line of code. Deliverable: clear scope.
analysis.description: We understand your idea, your process and what needs to accelerate. We review the context, goals and real priorities before proposing anything. This way we avoid building on assumptions and start from a clear scope.

study.title: Initial study
study.front: A first diagnostic and fit session before deciding.
study.back: A session to understand goal, context and fit. Deliverable: initial synthesis of approach and next phase.
study.description: We outline a first visual or functional direction before moving forward. We look at references, needs and possible approaches for your project. The result is a concrete foundation to decide on with confidence.

proposal.title: Proposal / approach
proposal.front: Scope, approach and direction defined from the study.
proposal.back: A development proposal with clear scope, approach and direction. Deliverable: clear proposal.
proposal.description: We define scope, priorities and a realistic development roadmap. We agree what gets built first and why, with indicative timelines and justified technical decisions. You decide with the full picture in front of you.

development.title: Development
development.front: Build with supervised AI and internal tooling.
development.back: Development with supervised AI and senior technical control. Deliverable: visible iterations.
development.description: We build with supervised AI, technical judgement and visible deliveries. Every step is reviewed and adjusted along the way, keeping human control over key decisions. You follow progress through real iterations, not promises.

review.title: Review
review.back: Testing, technical review and final adjustments. Deliverable: validated version.
review.description: We test, adjust and validate the solution before publishing it. We carefully review functionality, performance and visual details. We only move forward once the result meets what was agreed.

delivery.title: Delivery
delivery.back: Secure deployment of the solution. Deliverable: solution in production.
delivery.description: We deploy the solution and leave a base ready to grow. We explain how it works and what can be extended later. From here, the project is live in production and ready to evolve.

bridge.label: Initial study
bridge.message: Before asking you to move forward, we'd rather show you how it could look.
bridge.support: We include a first study to analyse your idea and outline an initial direction before you decide.

technology.title: Current technology
technology.description: We use modern and scalable technologies.

web.title: Fast web development
web.summary: Modern, secure websites and platforms optimised to communicate better and evolve with your business.
problems: You need a professional website that explains your offer clearly and creates a strong first impression.
Your current website does not reflect your brand, loads slowly or does not support the user journey.
You want to launch a campaign, service or product and need a polished digital foundation.
You need performance, technical SEO and user experience from the beginning.
approach: We start with the page structure: goals, sections, content hierarchy and calls to action.
We design a careful responsive experience, with clear navigation and content ready to capture leads.
We build the base with performance, technical on-page SEO and accessibility good practices.
We review loading, forms, links and basic measurement before publishing or leaving it ready to grow.

apps.title: Custom management applications
apps.summary: Internal management platforms to organise jobs, orders, invoicing and processes, adapted to how each team really works.
problems: You manage jobs, orders or invoicing with spreadsheets, email or tools that no longer scale.
You need an internal management panel with users, statuses, filters and your own processes.
Your daily operation has specific rules that a generic tool does not solve clearly.
You need a maintainable platform that can grow with the business.
approach: We model the real processes: jobs, orders, statuses, users, permissions and the points where time is lost today.
We define screens and internal flows so each role sees what it needs and can work without detours.
We build maintainable modules with filters, actions, validations and traceability for daily work.
We test complete operational cases and leave a base ready for invoicing, reporting or integrations.

tools.title: Internal tools and admin panels
tools.summary: Private admin panels to manage content, clients, services and processes without constantly depending on code changes.
problems: Every content or configuration change depends on someone touching code.
You need a private panel to manage clients, services, projects or documentation.
Several people need to manage information with different roles and permissions.
You need to control your information without depending on constant technical support.
approach: We identify what the team needs to manage without touching code: content, services, projects, bookings or documentation.
We design CRUDs, permissions and statuses so the panel is useful, controlled and easy to maintain.
We refine validations, messages, navigation and list views so administration is quick.
We deliver a private panel connected to the website or existing system, giving the team autonomy.

ai.title: AI process automation
ai.summary: Automations and supervised AI to reduce repetitive tasks and speed up internal processes without losing human control.
problems: Your team repeats classification, drafting or information-preparation tasks.
You want to apply AI without losing control over criteria, data and final review.
You need to connect automations with the tools the business already uses.
You want to speed up processes without turning AI into a black box.
approach: We choose repetitive tasks where AI can help without replacing the team's judgement.
We define inputs, rules, limits and human review points to avoid opaque automations.
We connect the workflow with existing tools and log relevant results, errors or decisions.
We test with real cases, adjust prompts and rules, and leave a process that can be supervised.

crm.title: CRM, data and reporting
crm.summary: Custom CRMs, dashboards and reports to centralise customer and process information and make decisions with clearer data.
problems: Commercial information is scattered and it is hard to see the real status of each customer or process.
You need a CRM, dashboard or report adapted to how you actually sell or manage.
You want to connect CRM, metrics and processes without creating more manual work.
You need decisions based on more reliable and traceable data.
approach: We organise the data model: customers, opportunities, activity, statuses and metrics that are actually useful.
We design working views and dashboards so the team finds information without relying on scattered sheets.
We connect sources, filters and reports while caring for consistency, permissions and data quality.
We validate indicators with the team and leave a base ready for reporting evolution.

integrations.title: Digital integrations
integrations.summary: Connections between tools, APIs and platforms so your systems work together with less friction.
problems: Your tools do not share information and the team ends up duplicating tasks.
You need to connect forms, CRM, automations, reporting or external platforms.
You want to reduce manual steps without replacing your whole current system.
You need systems to exchange data with control and clarity.
approach: We map systems, APIs, forms and databases to understand what information must move and when.
We define data contracts, validations and error handling before automating the exchange.
We build connectors, webhooks or scheduled processes with logs that make incidents visible.
We test synchronisations with real cases and document what each integration does.

mvp.title: MVPs and prototypes
mvp.summary: First functional versions to validate approach, experience and feasibility before expanding scope.
problems: You have an idea but need to see it working before investing in a full version.
You want to test flows, screens or assumptions with a usable base.
You need a first organised version that can evolve if the approach fits.
You want to reduce uncertainty without building more than needed.
approach: We reduce the idea to a testable first version: goal, users, critical flow and scope limits.
We design screens or a functional prototype to validate the experience before expanding.
We build only what is needed to test the hypothesis on an ordered, reusable base.
We collect learning and propose the next iteration without turning the MVP into an oversized product.

evolution.title: Improving and evolving existing software
evolution.summary: We review existing software to improve structure, performance, security or design, and prepare it to keep growing.
problems: Your current app or website works, but has performance or maintenance issues.
You need to add new modules on top of an existing base.
The existing code is hard to maintain or has become disorganised over time.
You want to improve security, design or usability without rebuilding from scratch.
approach: We audit the existing base: performance, security, structure, dependencies and fragile points.
We prioritise improvements by impact and risk so changes do not break what already works.
We refactor, add modules or correct technical debt while keeping compatibility with the business.
We measure the improvement and leave a clearer evolution path for future phases.

fallback.title: Digital service
fallback.summary: A digital solution adapted to a concrete business need.
problems: You need to organise a digital need before building.
You want to understand which solution fits your current context.
You are looking for a clear base to move forward without unnecessary complexity.
You want a solution prepared to evolve with good judgement.
approach: We clarify the need and separate what is essential from what is secondary.
We define the flow, the data and the way to validate that the solution fits.
We build a clear first version that can be reviewed and evolved.
We deliver basic documentation and realistic next steps.

hero.title: Abaco Developments

heroTagline.title: Built with technical judgement.
heroTagline.subtitle: We design, review and deliver with method, not trial and error.

abacoDevelopments.title: Who Abaco Developments is
abacoDevelopments.text:
At Abaco Developments we build tailor-made digital solutions for companies that need to organize processes, improve how they work and make better use of technology.

We come from technology consulting, CRM, data, automation, analytics and digital development. That experience has let us work on projects where technology isn't an add-on, but a real tool to solve problems, connect information and help teams work with more clarity.

The way we work always starts from understanding each client's context first. We don't believe in generic solutions or in building for the sake of building. Before we build, we analyze what the company needs, which process it wants to improve and what kind of tool can add real value.

Over time, technology has changed and so have we. Today we combine our experience with new artificial intelligence and assisted-development tools to move faster, but always with technical judgement, human review and control over every important decision.

At Abaco Developments we build websites, custom applications, automations, CRM, integrations, MVPs and internal tools adapted to each project.

Our goal is to build useful, secure and maintainable technology. Clear, well-thought-out solutions, ready to help each company work better, save time and move forward on a stronger digital foundation.

abacoQD.title: What AbacoQD is
abacoQD.text:
AbacoQD is born as a new way to explain and frame part of our work at Abaco Developments: the fast development of tailor-made digital solutions.

It stands for Ábaco Quick Developments and represents a more agile, modern and direct way of building digital projects. With AbacoQD we want to help companies that need to move fast, but without giving up order, security and technical judgement.

We work with new artificial intelligence and assisted-development tools to speed up phases of the process such as analysis, documentation, structure creation, programming or review. But we don't see AI as a replacement for technical work, rather as a tool that lets us be more efficient when used with supervision and good sense.

At AbacoQD we build websites, custom applications, automations, integrations, CRM, MVPs and internal tools designed to solve specific needs.

Our goal is to reduce times, adjust costs and create solutions better adapted to each client, always keeping a clear, reviewed way of working that's ready to grow.

contact.title: Contact details
contact.intro: Holder and contact details for legal purposes:

notice.metaDescription: Information about the site owner, terms of use, intellectual property and liability of Abaco Developments.
notice.title: Legal notice
notice.subtitle: Information about the site owner, terms of use and liability.

blocks.text: In compliance with the information duty set out in the applicable regulations on information society services and electronic commerce (LSSI-CE), the following details of the owner of this website are provided:

items: Owner: ABACO DIGITAL DEVELOPMENTS, S.L. (hereinafter, ABACO).
Tax ID (CIF): B-88229364.
Address: Calle Núñez de Balboa 35 A, Floor 5, Office A1, 28001 Madrid, Spain.
Registry details: Commercial Registry of Madrid, Volume 38273, Folio 65, Section GNE, Sheet No. 681002.
Email: info@abacodev.com.
Website: https://abacoqd.com/.

blocks.text: The official website of ABACO is https://abacoqd.com/.
This legal notice governs access to, navigation of and use of the website https://abacoqd.com/, without prejudice to ABACO setting specific terms for particular services.
The site aims to present ABACO's activity in custom software development, digital solutions and related services, and to facilitate contact with interested parties.

User status is acquired simply by accessing and browsing the site. The user undertakes to make diligent, correct and lawful use of the contents and services, in accordance with the law, this legal notice and good practice.
Access to the site is generally free of charge, except for the cost of the connection through the telecommunications network contracted by the user.

It is prohibited to use the site for unlawful purposes or purposes harmful to ABACO or third parties, or that may in any way damage, disable or impair the site or its services. In particular, it is prohibited to:

items: Introduce or distribute content that infringes the rights of third parties or applicable law.
Introduce programs, viruses or any element capable of causing damage to systems.
Attempt unauthorized access to restricted areas, accounts or systems of the site.
Carry out actions that cause a disproportionate overload of the site's infrastructure.

blocks.text: All site contents (texts, images, trademarks, logos, design, code and other elements) are owned by ABACO or by third parties who have authorized their use, and are protected by intellectual and industrial property regulations.

Reproduction, distribution, public communication, transformation or any other form of exploitation of the contents is not permitted without prior written authorization from ABACO, except for legally permitted uses.
The site may contain links to third-party sites. ABACO assumes no responsibility for the contents, policies or practices of those sites, access to which is the user's responsibility.

Anyone wishing to set up a link to this site must link to the home page and not make false statements or statements that may mislead or harm ABACO's image. Setting up the link does not imply any relationship, sponsorship or approval by ABACO.
ABACO works to keep the site available and up to date, but does not guarantee permanent availability and continuity of the site or the absence of errors in its contents.
To the extent permitted by law, ABACO shall not be liable for damages arising from use of the site, lack of technical availability, or misuse by third parties.
This legal notice is governed by Spanish law. For the resolution of any dispute, the parties shall submit to the courts and tribunals that are competent under the applicable regulations.
For any query relating to this legal notice you can contact ABACO at info@abacodev.com or at the postal address indicated in the owner identification details.

privacy.metaDescription: Information on how Abaco Developments processes personal data received through the site, in accordance with the GDPR and the LOPDGDD.
privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: Information about how we process the personal data received through the site.

blocks.text: The controller of the personal data collected through this site is ABACO DIGITAL DEVELOPMENTS, S.L. (ABACO), Tax ID B-88229364, with address at Calle Núñez de Balboa 35 A, Floor 5, Office A1, 28001 Madrid. Privacy contact email: info@abacodev.com.
Depending on the user's interaction, ABACO may process the following categories of data:

items: Identification and contact data: name, email, phone and company, when provided.

Content of communications: the information the user includes in their messages or requests, including booking requests (name, email, phone, company, requested date and time, and any associated message).
Consent data: acceptance date and time of the privacy policy and, where applicable, separate consent for commercial communications.
Technical data necessary for security and operation: IP address, browser (user agent), device information, and date and time of access.

blocks.text: Only the data the user voluntarily provides, or that is necessary to deliver the requested service, is processed.
Data is processed for the following purposes:

items: Handling queries and requests sent through the contact form.
Managing booking or appointment requests.
Sending commercial communications only when the user has specifically consented.
Maintaining the security and proper operation of the site.

blocks.text: Processing is based, as applicable, on:

items: The consent of the data subject (for example, when submitting forms or subscribing to communications).
The performance of pre-contractual measures or the requested relationship.
ABACO's legitimate interest in responding to queries and ensuring the security of the site.
Compliance with applicable legal obligations.

blocks.text: The contact form collects the data necessary to handle the request. Acceptance of the privacy policy is mandatory to submit it; commercial communications are always optional and collected via a separate checkbox, never combined with the mandatory acceptance.
When the user requests a meeting or appointment, the data necessary to manage and confirm the booking is processed. This data is used solely for that purpose.

If the user expressly consents, ABACO may send communications about services, news or content. Consent may be withdrawn at any time, without retroactive effect, through the means indicated or the unsubscribe link included in the communications.

Data will be kept for as long as it is necessary for the purpose for which it was collected and, thereafter, for the legally required periods. When it is no longer necessary, it will be deleted using appropriate security measures.

In general, data is not shared with third parties except where legally required. To provide the service, technology providers necessary for hosting, maintenance or operation of the site may be involved, acting as data processors under the corresponding agreements where applicable.
No international data transfers are planned; if they become necessary in the future, prior information will be given and appropriate safeguards will be adopted.
Users may exercise the following rights over their data:

items: Access to their personal data.
Rectification of inaccurate data.
Erasure of the data.
Objection to processing.
Restriction of processing.
Data portability.
Withdrawal of consent given, where processing is based on it.

blocks.text: To exercise these rights you may send a request to info@abacodev.com or to the postal address Calle Núñez de Balboa 35 A, Floor 5, Office A1, 28001 Madrid, indicating the right you wish to exercise. ABACO may request proof of identity where necessary.
If you consider that the processing of your data does not comply with the regulations, you have the right to file a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD).
ABACO applies reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, loss or alteration, in line with the state of the art.
This policy may be updated to adapt it to regulatory or operational changes of the site. We recommend reviewing it periodically; the last update date is shown at the top.
For any matter relating to privacy you can contact ABACO at info@abacodev.com.

cookies.metaDescription: Information about the technical cookies and storage technologies required for the operation of the site.
cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.subtitle: Information about the technical cookies and storage technologies required for the operation of the site.

blocks.text: Cookies are small files that a website stores on the user's device. Alongside them there are other local storage technologies (such as localStorage) that perform similar functions to remember preferences.

sections.heading: Types of cookies that may exist

blocks.text: Depending on their purpose, the following can be distinguished, among others:

items: Technical or necessary cookies: essential for the operation of the site.
Preference cookies: remember the user's options, such as language or theme.
Analytics cookies: this site does not use analytics cookies.
Third-party cookies: this site does not use third-party cookies, heatmaps, advertising tools or external tracking.

blocks.text: This site currently uses only its own technical and preference storage. The following table summarizes the technologies used:

rows: Remember the selected light, dark or system mode.
Persistent until changed or the browser is cleared.
Persistent until changed or the browser is cleared.
Persistent until changed or the browser is cleared.
Security, session and CSRF protection or technical operation of the site.
Session or the technically necessary period.

blocks.text: Technical or necessary cookies and technologies enable navigation and use of the basic functions of the site. As they are essential, they do not require prior consent.

To remember the theme (light, dark or system), the language and accessibility settings, the site uses the browser's own local storage. This information stays on the device until it is changed or cleared.
This site does not use analytics cookies, heatmaps, advertising tools, tracking pixels or external measurement providers.

sections.heading: How to configure or delete cookies

blocks.text: The user can configure or delete cookies and local storage from their browser options. Disabling some technical technologies may affect the operation of the site.
This cookie policy may be updated when the technologies used or the applicable regulations change. The last update date is shown at the top.
For any questions about the use of cookies you can contact ABACO at info@abacodev.com.
